evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Establishing standards for data modeling, testing, and metadata management
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (testing standards, metadata management, data quality monitoring, best practices, attention to detail) dominate with no velocity/shipping-speed language present anywhere in the JD.
evidence.domain_risk: Building "robust pipelines and data models downstream of backend services (mostly in BigQuery)" supporting reporting and financial use cases
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Monzo is a regulated UK bank and the role explicitly supports financial use cases and reporting, matching high domain risk via the default-high fintech/banking sector plus explicit financial-reporting language.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Optimizing data warehouse architecture to reduce complexity and costs; Establishing standards for data modeling, testing, and metadata management
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The role optimizes an existing, complex data warehouse architecture and establishes org-wide standards, indicating an established, specialised data function operating at scale.
evidence.jd_authorship: Building "robust pipelines and data models downstream of backend services (mostly in BigQuery)" supporting reporting and financial use cases
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities name a specific tool (BigQuery) and precise application context (downstream of backend services, financial use cases), letting the reader understand the actual day-to-day work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: supporting reporting and financial use cases
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The explicit application domain is financial reporting and use cases at a bank, matching finance orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Optimizing data warehouse architecture to reduce complexity and costs; Establishing standards for data modeling, testing, and metadata management
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role combines strategic ownership of architecture and standards-setting with scoped pipeline-building execution work, giving a mixed autonomy profile.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate's own work or AI systems consuming the candidate's data output.
evidence.testing_framing: Establishing standards for data modeling, testing, and metadata management
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing is framed as a standard the candidate defines and establishes across the team, matching ownership-level responsibility rather than a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Collaborating with colleagues to monitor and enhance data quality
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: As a regulated bank with pipelines feeding financial reporting and use cases, combined with repeated data quality/testing/standards language throughout, this reflects a high emphasis on preventing bad data from reaching financial decision-making.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Optimizing data warehouse architecture to reduce complexity and costs
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role optimizes and reduces complexity in an existing data warehouse architecture rather than building new infrastructure from scratch, matching fix/scale.
evidence.work_arrangement: Flexible UK-based remote work with occasional London office meetings (Location: Cardiff, London, or Remote (UK))
evidence.loss_aversion: "supporting reporting and financial use cases" at a regulated UK bank, combined with repeated emphasis on testing standards and data quality monitoring, signals high loss-aversion framing centered on financial data trustworthiness.
